# Scaffold a New infra-live Repository

This runbook creates a new **infra-live** repository following the [Gruntwork infrastructure-live pattern](https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt-infrastructure-live-stacks-example). The generated repo includes:

- **`root.hcl`** — Shared Terragrunt configuration with AWS provider, S3 remote state, and catalog settings
- **`mise.toml`** — Pinned versions of OpenTofu and Terragrunt
- **`.gitignore`** — Standard ignores for Terragrunt, OpenTofu, and IDE files
- **`README.md`** — Documents the folder hierarchy and how to add accounts and modules

The repo will be ready for you to add AWS accounts and deploy modules once your accounts are created.

## Pre-flight Checks

### Authenticate to GitHub

<GitHubAuth
  id="gh-auth"
  title="Authenticate to GitHub"
  description="Authenticate to GitHub to create a repository and push the scaffolded files."
/>

### Install mise

<Check
  id="check-mise"
  command="mise --version && mise self-update --yes;"
  title="Check mise Installation"
  description="Verify that `mise` is installed and up to date."
  successMessage="mise is installed and up to date!"
  failMessage="mise is not installed. Install it from https://mise.jdx.dev/getting-started.html"
/>

## Create the Repository

### Name your repository

<Inputs id="repo-config">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: OrgName
    type: string
    description: GitHub organization or user that owns this repo
    validations:
      - required
  - name: RepoName
    type: string
    description: Name of the infra-live repository
    default: infra-live
    validations:
      - required
```
</Inputs>

### Create a new GitHub repo

Create a new empty repository on GitHub. The scaffolded files will be pushed to it.

<Command
  id="create-repo"
  path="scripts/create-repo.sh"
  title="Create GitHub Repository"
  description="Create a new GitHub repository for infra-live."
  successMessage="Repository created!"
  failMessage="Failed to create repository. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId="repo-config"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

### Clone the repository

<GitClone
  id="clone-repo"
  gitHubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone the new repository"
  description="Clone the empty repository so we can scaffold files into it."
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/{{ .inputs.OrgName }}/{{ .inputs.RepoName }}"
  inputsId="repo-config"
/>

## Configure and Generate Files

### Look up latest tool versions

<Command
  id="lookup-versions"
  path="scripts/lookup-versions.sh"
  title="Look Up Latest Versions"
  description="Fetch the latest OpenTofu and Terragrunt versions from GitHub."
  successMessage="Versions fetched! Enter them in the form below."
  failMessage="Failed to look up versions. You can enter them manually."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

### Configure and generate the scaffold

<Template
  id="scaffold"
  path="templates/infra-live-scaffold"
  target="worktree"
  inputsId="repo-config"
/>

## Push the Scaffolded Files

Create a pull request with the generated files.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-scaffold"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  inputsId="scaffold"
  title="Create Pull Request"
  description="Push the scaffolded infra-live files and open a PR."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Scaffold infra-live repository"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="Initial scaffold of the infra-live repository.\n\nIncludes:\n- root.hcl (Terragrunt root config)\n- mise.toml (pinned tool versions)\n- .gitignore\n- README.md\n\nGenerated by the scaffold-infra-live runbook."
  prefilledBranchName="scaffold-infra-live"
/>

### Merge the pull request

Review and merge the pull request on GitHub, then verify it's been merged.

<Check
  id="check-pr-merged"
  path="checks/check-pr-merged.sh"
  title="Verify PR is Merged"
  description="Check that the scaffold pull request has been merged to main."
  successMessage="PR is merged! Your infra-live repo is ready."
  failMessage="PR is not yet merged. Merge it on GitHub and try again."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

## Next Steps

Your infra-live repo is now scaffolded and ready. When you have AWS accounts to add:

1. Create account folders (e.g., `dev/`, `staging/`, `prod/`)
2. Add an `account.hcl` in each with the account name and ID
3. Add region folders (e.g., `dev/us-east-1/`) with a `region.hcl`
4. Deploy modules by adding `terragrunt.hcl` files that source from your infra-catalog

See the generated `README.md` in your repo for the full folder hierarchy guide.
